channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis of RCTs found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction offered additional benefits over low-load exercise and comparable efficacy to high-load exercise in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain intensity in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found high agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions for musculoskeletal dysfunctions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared to senior physicians in an emergency department setting.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: A systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis, including randomized controlled trials with EDSS scores ≤6.0, but found effects remain unclear due to narrative synthesis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A validation study compared the accuracy of stride-count measurement across multiple wearable sensor configurations (ActiGraph accelerometers on wrists, hips, ankles, and pressure insoles) in 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults against a gold-standard instrumented treadmill, finding varying accuracy based on placement and algorithm.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: This study developed a core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) and corresponding measures (PROMs) for pediatric physical therapy, grouped into three domains (functioning, wellbeing, context), suitable for daily practice, research, and quality assessment.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Impact of Mandatory Bundled Payment on Prehabilitation Delivery for Lower Extremity Joint Replacement.
item.description: A difference-in-differences analysis found that the implementation of the Comprehensive Care for Joint Replacement (CJR) mandatory bundled payment model negatively impacted the proportion of hospitals delivering prehabilitation for lower extremity joint replacement in the US.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Follow up to the 2018 IOC consensus statement on paediatric ACL injuries: towards a broader vision of care
item.description: This editorial follows up on the 2018 IOC consensus statement on pediatric ACL injuries, examining recent advances and challenges in prevention, individualized management decisions, evolving surgical strategies, and long-term holistic care.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Multimodal Integration of Gait, Balance, and Infrared Thermography Enhances Machine-Learning Classification of Knee Osteoarthritis: A Cross-Sectional Study
item.description: A cross-sectional study found that multimodal integration of gait, balance, and infrared thermography enhanced machine-learning classification of knee osteoarthritis compared to single-domain models.
item.author: Archives of PM&R

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial of 140 TIA and mild stroke participants found no significant difference in functional exercise capacity, physical activity, anthropometrics, blood pressure, or quality-of-life over 6 months between integrated cardiovascular rehabilitation and usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating in athletes: a systematic review and meta-analysis
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis estimated the prevalence of eating disorders and disordered eating among athletes across training levels and assessment methods, identifying associated moderators.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study of 137 patients found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model exceeded national completion benchmarks (70.9% vs. 43%), with 96% of completers achieving minimal clinically important differences.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Early Weight-Bearing Speeds Recovery After Unstable Ankle Fracture Surgery: study
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found that early weight-bearing and mobilization significantly speed recovery, improve functional outcomes, and progress rehabilitation after surgical fixation of unstable ankle fractures, without increasing complication rates.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse: A systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials
item.description: This systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials confirms the efficacy and safety of pelvic floor muscle training for pelvic organ prolapse.
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review compared upper limb rehabilitation strategies and outcomes in chronic stroke patients with and without contralateral C7 nerve transfer, identifying key evidence gaps in rehabilitation dose, content, and delivery models.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Mental health in tennis: a cross-organisational hybrid summit-Delphi consensus process
item.description: A multi-method consensus process, involving a summit and Delphi process, generated recommendations for the tennis community regarding mental healthcare provision and mental wellness promotion among elite tennis athletes, including juniors.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Subtype-specific optimal cut-off values of the Berg Balance Scale for predicting independent walking in inpatient stroke rehabilitation: a multicentre cohort study.
item.description: A multicenter retrospective cohort study of 565 stroke patients in South Korea derived subtype-specific optimal cut-off values for the Berg Balance Scale to predict independent walking at discharge.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A systematic qualitative evidence synthesis of 22 studies (645 informants) identified two overarching domains of psychosocial impacts—psychological and social—related to eating and drinking difficulties after stroke.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: An exploratory retrospective cohort study of 333 adult patients undergoing elective ACDF found that sarcopenia increases the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of index surgery.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: A pragmatic quasi-experimental study evaluated the effectiveness of a 12-week exergaming intervention (Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure) on physical, psychological, and social outcomes in 72 institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia in Taiwan.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Incidence of injury and illness at the Paris 2024 Paralympic Games: post-pandemic trends and sport-specific risks
item.description: A prospective observational study monitored 4450 athletes during the 14-day Paris 2024 Paralympic Games, reporting 535 injuries (prevalence = 10.9%) with an incidence of 8.6 per 1000 athlete days.
item.author: BJSM
